member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.AllowNullAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that null is allowed as an input even if the corresponding type disallows it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DisallowNullAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that null is disallowed as an input even if the corresponding type allows it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MaybeNullAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that an output may be null even if the corresponding type disallows it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that an output will not be null even if the corresponding type allows it. Specifies that an input argument was not null when the call returns.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MaybeNullWhenAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that when a method returns , the parameter may be null even if the corresponding type disallows it.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MaybeNullWhenAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the specified return value condition.
member.param: The return value condition. If the method returns this value, the associated parameter may be .

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MaybeNullWhenAttribute.ReturnValue
member.summary: Gets the return value condition.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullWhenAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that when a method returns , the parameter will not be null even if the corresponding type allows it.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullWhenAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the specified return value condition.
member.param: The return value condition. If the method returns this value, the associated parameter will not be .

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullWhenAttribute.ReturnValue
member.summary: Gets the return value condition.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullIfNotNullAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that the output will be non-null if the named parameter is non-null.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullIfNotNullAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the associated parameter name.
member.param: The associated parameter name. The output will be non-null if the argument to the parameter specified is non-null.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullIfNotNullAttribute.ParameterName
member.summary: Gets the associated parameter name.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DoesNotReturnAttribute
member.summary: Applied to a method that will never return under any circumstance.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DoesNotReturnIfAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that the method will not return if the associated Boolean parameter is passed the specified value.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DoesNotReturnIfAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the specified parameter value.
member.param: The condition parameter value. Code after the method will be considered unreachable by diagnostics if the argument to the associated parameter matches this value.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DoesNotReturnIfAttribute.ParameterValue
member.summary: Gets the condition parameter value.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that the method or property will ensure that the listed field and property members have not-null values.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with a field or property member.
member.param: The field or property member that is promised to be not-null.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullAttribute.#ctor(System.String[])
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the list of field and property members.
member.param: The list of field and property members that are promised to be not-null.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullAttribute.Members
member.summary: Gets field or property member names.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullWhenAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that the method or property will ensure that the listed field and property members have not-null values when returning with the specified return value condition.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullWhenAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean,System.String)
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the specified return value condition and a field or property member.
member.param: The return value condition. If the method returns this value, the associated parameter will not be .
The field or property member that is promised to be not-null.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullWhenAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean,System.String[])
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the specified return value condition and list of field and property members.
member.param: The return value condition. If the method returns this value, the associated parameter will not be .
The list of field and property members that are promised to be not-null.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullWhenAttribute.ReturnValue
member.summary: Gets the return value condition.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullWhenAttribute.Members
member.summary: Gets field or property member names.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMembersAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that certain members on a specified are accessed dynamically, for example through .
member.remarks:
This allows tools to understand which members are being accessed during the execution of a program.
This attribute is valid on members whose type is or .
When this attribute is applied to a location of type , the assumption is that the string represents a fully qualified type name.
When this attribute is applied to a class, interface, or struct, the members specified can be accessed dynamically on instances returned from calling on instances of that class, interface, or struct.
If the attribute is applied to a method it's treated as a special case and it implies the attribute should be applied to the "this" parameter of the method. As such the attribute should only be used on instance methods of types assignable to System.Type (or string, but no methods will use it there).

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMembersAttribute.#ctor(System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified member types.
member.param: The types of members dynamically accessed.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMembersAttribute.MemberTypes
member.summary: Gets the which specifies the type of members dynamically accessed.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes
member.summary:
Specifies the types of members that are dynamically accessed.
This enumeration has a attribute that allows a bitwise combination of its member values.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.None
member.summary: Specifies no members.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicParameterlessConstructor
member.summary: Specifies the default, parameterless public constructor.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicConstructors
member.summary: Specifies all public constructors.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicConstructors
member.summary: Specifies all non-public constructors.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicMethods
member.summary: Specifies all public methods.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicMethods
member.summary: Specifies all non-public methods.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicFields
member.summary: Specifies all public fields.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicFields
member.summary: Specifies all non-public fields.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicNestedTypes
member.summary: Specifies all public nested types.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicNestedTypes
member.summary: Specifies all non-public nested types.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicProperties
member.summary: Specifies all public properties.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicProperties
member.summary: Specifies all non-public properties.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicEvents
member.summary: Specifies all public events.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicEvents
member.summary: Specifies all non-public events.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.Interfaces
member.summary: Specifies all interfaces implemented by the type.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All
member.summary: Specifies all members.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute
member.summary: States a dependency that one member has on another.
member.remarks: This can be used to inform tooling of a dependency that is otherwise not evident purely from metadata and IL, for example a member relied on via reflection.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified signature of a member on the same type as the consumer.
member.param: The signature of the member depended on.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.#ctor(System.String,System.Type)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified signature of a member on a .
member.param: The signature of the member depended on.
The containing .

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified signature of a member on a type in an assembly.
member.param: The signature of the member depended on.
The full name of the type containing the specified member.
The assembly name of the type containing the specified member.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.#ctor(System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes,System.Type)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified types of members on a .
member.param: The types of members depended on.
The containing the specified members.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.#ctor(System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified types of members on a type in an assembly.
member.param: The types of members depended on.
The full name of the type containing the specified members.
The assembly name of the type containing the specified members.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.MemberSignature
member.summary: Gets the signature of the member depended on.
member.remarks: Either must be a valid string or must not equal , but not both.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.MemberTypes
member.summary: Gets the which specifies the type of members depended on.
member.remarks: Either must be a valid string or must not equal , but not both.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.Type
member.summary: Gets the containing the specified member.
member.remarks: If neither nor are specified, the type of the consumer is assumed.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.TypeName
member.summary: Gets the full name of the type containing the specified member.
member.remarks: If neither nor are specified, the type of the consumer is assumed.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.AssemblyName
member.summary: Gets the assembly name of the specified type.
member.remarks: is only valid when is specified.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.Condition
member.summary: Gets or sets the condition in which the dependency is applicable, e.g. "DEBUG".

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresAssemblyFilesAttribute
member.summary: /// Indicates that the specified member requires assembly files to be on disk.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresAssemblyFilesAttribute.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresAssemblyFilesAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: A message that contains information about the need for assembly files to be on disk.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresAssemblyFilesAttribute.Message
member.summary: Gets an optional message that contains information about the need for assembly files to be on disk.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresAssemblyFilesAttribute.Url
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional URL that contains more information about the member, why it requires assembly files to be on disk, and what options a consumer has to deal with it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the specified method requires the ability to generate new code at runtime, for example through .
member.remarks: This allows tools to understand which methods are unsafe to call when compiling ahead of time.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified message.
member.param: A message that contains information about the usage of dynamic code.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute.Message
member.summary: Gets a message that contains information about the usage of dynamic code.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute.Url
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional URL that contains more information about the method, why it requires dynamic code, and what options a consumer has to deal with it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresUnreferencedCodeAttribute
member.summary: /// Indicates that the specified method requires dynamic access to code that is not referenced statically, for example through .
member.remarks: This allows tools to understand which methods are unsafe to call when removing unreferenced code from an application.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresUnreferencedCodeAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified message.
member.param: A message that contains information about the usage of unreferenced code.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresUnreferencedCodeAttribute.Message
member.summary: Gets a message that contains information about the usage of unreferenced code.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresUnreferencedCodeAttribute.Url
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional URL that contains more information about the method, why it requires unreferenced code, and what options a consumer has to deal with it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute
member.summary: /// Suppresses reporting of a specific rule violation, allowing multiple suppressions on a single code artifact.
member.remarks: is different than in that it doesn't have a . So it is always preserved in the compiled assembly.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.#ctor(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class, specifying the category of the tool and the identifier for an analysis rule.
member.param: The category for the attribute.
The identifier of the analysis rule the attribute applies to.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Category
member.summary: Gets the category identifying the classification of the attribute.
member.remarks: The property describes the tool or tool analysis category for which a message suppression attribute applies.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.CheckId
member.summary: Gets the identifier of the analysis tool rule to be suppressed.
member.remarks: Concatenated together, the and properties form a unique check identifier.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Scope
member.summary: Gets or sets the scope of the code that is relevant for the attribute.
member.remarks: The Scope property is an optional argument that specifies the metadata scope for which the attribute is relevant.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Target
member.summary: Gets or sets a fully qualified path that represents the target of the attribute.
member.remarks: The property is an optional argument identifying the analysis target of the attribute. An example value is "System.IO.Stream.ctor():System.Void". Because it is fully qualified, it can be long, particularly for targets such as parameters. The analysis tool user interface should be capable of automatically formatting the parameter.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.MessageId
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional argument expanding on exclusion criteria.
member.remarks: The property is an optional argument that specifies additional exclusion where the literal metadata target is not sufficiently precise. For example, the cannot be applied within a method, and it may be desirable to suppress a violation against a statement in the method that will give a rule violation, but not against all statements in the method.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Justification
member.summary: Gets or sets the justification for suppressing the code analysis message.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ExperimentalAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that an API element is experimental and subject to change without notice.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ExperimentalAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: Human readable explanation for marking experimental API.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ExperimentalAttribute.DiagnosticId
member.summary: Gets the ID that the compiler will use when reporting a use of the API the attribute applies to.
member.remarks:
The diagnostic ID is shown in build output for warnings and errors.
This property represents the unique ID that can be used to suppress the warnings or errors, if needed.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ExperimentalAttribute.UrlFormat
member.summary: Gets or sets the URL for corresponding documentation. The API accepts a format string instead of an actual URL, creating a generic URL that includes the diagnostic ID.
member.value: The format string that represents a URL to corresponding documentation.
member.remarks: An example format string is https://contoso.com/obsoletion-warnings/{0}.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CallerArgumentExpressionAttribute
member.summary: Tags parameter that should be filled with specific caller name.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CallerArgumentExpressionAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: Function parameter to take the name from.

member.name: P:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CallerArgumentExpressionAttribute.ParameterName
member.summary: Gets name of the function parameter that name should be taken from.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.SkipLocalsInitAttribute
member.summary: Used to indicate to the compiler that the .locals init flag should not be set in method headers.
member.remarks: This attribute is unsafe because it may reveal uninitialized memory to the application in certain instances (e.g., reading from uninitialized stackalloc'd memory). If applied to a method directly, the attribute applies to that method and all nested functions (lambdas, local functions) below it. If applied to a type or module, it applies to all methods nested inside. This attribute is intentionally not permitted on assemblies. Use at the module level instead to apply to multiple type declarations.

member.name: T:System.Text.RedactionStringBuilderExtensions
member.summary: Redaction utility methods.

member.name: M:System.Text.RedactionStringBuilderExtensions.AppendRedacted(System.Text.StringBuilder,Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.Redactor,System.String)
member.summary: Redacts potentially sensitive data and appends it to a instance.
member.param: Instance of to append the redacted value.
The redactor that will redact the input value.
Value to redact.
member.remarks: When the is nothing will be appended to the string builder.

member.name: M:System.Text.RedactionStringBuilderExtensions.AppendRedacted(System.Text.StringBuilder,Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.Redactor,System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char})
member.summary: Redacts potentially sensitive data and appends it to a instance.
member.param: Instance of to append the redacted value.
The redactor that will redact the input value.
Value to redact.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.DiagnosticIds.DiagnosticIds
member.summary: Various diagnostic IDs reported by this repo.
member.remarks: When adding a new diagnostic ID, add a corresponding suppression to the root Directory.Build.targets file, and add a documentation entry to docs/list-of-diagnostics.md.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.DiagnosticIds.DiagnosticIds.Experiments
member.summary: Experiments supported by this repo.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw
member.summary: Defines static methods used to throw exceptions.
member.remarks: The main purpose is to reduce code size, improve performance, and standardize exception messages.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfNull``1(``0,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the specified argument is .
member.typeparam: Argument type to be checked for .
member.param: Object to be checked for .
The name of the parameter being checked.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfNullOrMemberNull``2(``0,``1,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the specified argument is , or if the specified member is .
member.typeparam: Argument type to be checked for .
Member type to be checked for .
member.param: Argument to be checked for .
Object member to be checked for .
The name of the parameter being checked.
The name of the member.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfMemberNull``2(``0,``1,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the specified member is .
member.typeparam: Argument type.
Member type to be checked for .
member.param: Argument to which member belongs.
Object member to be checked for .
The name of the parameter being checked.
The name of the member.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfNullOrWhitespace(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Throws either an or an if the specified string is or whitespace respectively.
member.param: String to be checked for or whitespace.
The name of the parameter being checked.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfNullOrEmpty(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the string is , or if it is empty.
member.param: String to be checked for or empty.
The name of the parameter being checked.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfBufferTooSmall(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the argument's buffer size is less than the required buffer size.
member.param: The actual buffer size.
The required buffer size.
The name of the parameter to be checked.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfOutOfRange``1(``0,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the enum value is not valid.
member.param: The argument to evaluate.
The name of the parameter being checked.
member.typeparam: The type of the enumeration.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfNullOrEmpty``1(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the collection is , or if it is empty.
member.param: The collection to evaluate.
The name of the parameter being checked.
member.typeparam: The type of objects in the collection.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.ArgumentNullException(System.String)
member.summary: Throws an .
member.param: The name of the parameter that caused the exception.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.ArgumentNullException(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an .
member.param: The name of the parameter that caused the exception.
A message that describes the error.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.ArgumentOutOfRangeException(System.String)
member.summary: Throws an .
member.param: The name of the parameter that caused the exception.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.ArgumentOutOfRangeException(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an .
member.param: The name of the parameter that caused the exception.
A message that describes the error.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.ArgumentOutOfRangeException(System.String,System.Object,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an .
member.param: The name of the parameter that caused the exception.
The value of the argument that caused this exception.
A message that describes the error.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.ArgumentException(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an .
member.param: The name of the parameter that caused the exception.
A message that describes the error.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.ArgumentException(System.String,System.String,System.Exception)
member.summary: Throws an .
member.param: The name of the parameter that caused the exception.
A message that describes the error.
The exception that is the cause of the current exception.
member.remarks: If the is not a , the current exception is raised in a catch block that handles the inner exception.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.InvalidOperationException(System.String)
member.summary: Throws an .
member.param: A message that describes the error.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.InvalidOperationException(System.String,System.Exception)
member.summary: Throws an .
member.param: A message that describes the error.
The exception that is the cause of the current exception.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfLessThan(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the specified number is less than min.
member.param: Number to be expected being less than min.
The number that must be less than the argument.
The name of the parameter being checked.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfGreaterThan(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the specified number is greater than max.
member.param: Number to be expected being greater than max.
The number that must be greater than the argument.
The name of the parameter being checked.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfLessThanOrEqual(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the specified number is less or equal than min.
member.param: Number to be expected being less or equal than min.
The number that must be less or equal than the argument.
The name of the parameter being checked.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfGreaterThanOrEqual(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the specified number is greater or equal than max.
member.param: Number to be expected being greater or equal than max.
The number that must be greater or equal than the argument.
The name of the parameter being checked.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfOutOfRange(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the specified number is not in the specified range.
member.param: Number to be expected being greater or equal than max.
The lower bound of the allowed range of argument values.
The upper bound of the allowed range of argument values.
The name of the parameter being checked.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfZero(System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the specified number is equal to 0.
member.param: Number to be expected being not equal to zero.
The name of the parameter being checked.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfLessThan(System.UInt32,System.UInt32,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the specified number is less than min.
member.param: Number to be expected being less than min.
The number that must be less than the argument.
The name of the parameter being checked.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfGreaterThan(System.UInt32,System.UInt32,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the specified number is greater than max.
member.param: Number to be expected being greater than max.
The number that must be greater than the argument.
The name of the parameter being checked.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfLessThanOrEqual(System.UInt32,System.UInt32,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the specified number is less or equal than min.
member.param: Number to be expected being less or equal than min.
The number that must be less or equal than the argument.
The name of the parameter being checked.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfGreaterThanOrEqual(System.UInt32,System.UInt32,System.String)

member.param: Number to be expected being greater or equal than max.
The number that must be greater or equal than the argument.
The name of the parameter being checked.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfOutOfRange(System.UInt32,System.UInt32,System.UInt32,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the specified number is not in the specified range.
member.param: Number to be expected being greater or equal than max.
The lower bound of the allowed range of argument values.
The upper bound of the allowed range of argument values.
The name of the parameter being checked.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfZero(System.UInt32,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the specified number is equal to 0.
member.param: Number to be expected being not equal to zero.
The name of the parameter being checked.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfLessThan(System.Int64,System.Int64,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the specified number is less than min.
member.param: Number to be expected being less than min.
The number that must be less than the argument.
The name of the parameter being checked.

member.summary: Throws an if the specified number is greater than max.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.RentedSpan`1
member.summary: Represents a span that's potentially created over a rented array.
member.typeparam: The type of objects held by the span.
member.remarks:
This type is used to implement a common pattern to improve performance when using temporary buffers. The pattern encourages the use of stack-based buffers when possible, which eliminate the overhead of garbage collection of buffers.
With this type, you make a speculative call to allocate a buffer. If the buffer is too large to fix on the stack, it is allocated from . If the buffer could be allocated on the stack, then no buffer is acquired from the array pool, and instead the caller is expected to use to get the buffer.

example.code: using var rental = new RentedSpan<char>(length); var span = rental.Rented ? rental.Span : stackalloc char[length];

member.name: F:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.RentedSpan`1.MinimumRentalSpace
member.summary: The minimum size in bytes that triggers buffer rental.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.RentedSpan`1.#ctor(System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the struct.
member.param: The desired length of the span. if this value is %lt;= 0, no buffer is allocated.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.RentedSpan`1.Dispose
member.summary: Returns a rented array back to the array pool.
member.remarks:
If no array was actually allocated from the array pool (when is ), then this method has no effect.
Calling this method multiple times on the same object is not supported, don't do it.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.RentedSpan`1.Span
member.summary: Gets a span over the rented buffer.
member.remarks: If no buffer was rented (because the buffer was deemed too small), then this returns an empty span. When a buffer isn't rented by this type, it's a cue to you to allocate buffer from the stack instead using stackalloc.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.RentedSpan`1.Rented
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether a buffer has been rented.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.NoopPooledObjectPolicy`1
member.summary: An object pool policy that does nothing.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PooledCancellationTokenSourcePolicy
member.summary: An object pool policy for cancellation token sources.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PooledDictionaryPolicy`2
member.summary: An object pool policy for dictionaries.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PooledListPolicy`1
member.summary: An object pool policy for lists.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PooledListWithCapacityPolicy`1
member.summary: An object pool policy for lists with capacity.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PooledSetPolicy`1
member.summary: An object pool policy for sets.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PoolFactory
member.summary: A factory of object pools.
member.remarks: This class makes it easy to create efficient object pools used to improve performance by reducing strain on the garbage collector.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PoolFactory.CreatePool``1(System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates an object pool.
member.typeparam: The type of object to keep in the pool.
member.param: The maximum number of items to keep in the pool. This defaults to 1024. This value is a recommendation, the pool may keep more objects than this.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PoolFactory.CreatePool``1(Microsoft.Extensions.ObjectPool.IPooledObjectPolicy{``0},System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates an object pool with a custom policy.
member.typeparam: The type of object to keep in the pool.
member.param: The custom policy that is responsible for creating new objects and preparing objects to be added to the pool.
The maximum number of items to keep in the pool. This defaults to 1024. This value is a recommendation, the pool may keep more objects than this.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PoolFactory.CreateResettingPool``1(System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates an object pool for resettable objects.
member.typeparam: The type of object to keep in the pool.
member.param: The maximum number of items to keep in the pool. This defaults to 1024. This value is a recommendation, the pool may keep more objects than this.
member.remarks: Objects are systematically reset before being added to the pool.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PoolFactory.CreateStringBuilderPool(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates a pool of instances.
member.param: The maximum number of items to keep in the pool. This defaults to 1024. This value is a recommendation, the pool may keep more objects than this.
The maximum capacity of the string builders to keep in the pool. This defaults to 64K.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PoolFactory.CreateListPool``1(System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates an object pool of instances.
member.typeparam: The type of object held by the lists.
member.param: The maximum number of items to keep in the pool. This defaults to 1024. This value is a recommendation, the pool may keep more objects than this.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PoolFactory.CreateListPoolWithCapacity``1(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates an object pool of instances, each with provided .
member.typeparam: The type of object held by the lists.
member.param: The capacity of each created instance.
The maximum number of items to keep in the pool. This defaults to 1024. This value is a recommendation, the pool may keep more objects than this.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PoolFactory.CreateDictionaryPool``2(System.Collections.Generic.IEqualityComparer{``0},System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates an object pool of instances.
member.typeparam: The type of the dictionary keys.
The type of the dictionary values.
member.param: Optional key comparer used by the dictionaries.
The maximum number of items to keep in the pool. This defaults to 1024. This value is a recommendation, the pool may keep more objects than this.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PoolFactory.CreateHashSetPool``1(System.Collections.Generic.IEqualityComparer{``0},System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates an object pool of instances.
member.typeparam: The type of objects held in the sets.
member.param: Optional key comparer used by the sets.
The maximum number of items to keep in the pool. This defaults to 1024. This value is a recommendation, the pool may keep more objects than this.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PoolFactory.CreateCancellationTokenSourcePool(System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates an object pool of instances.
member.param: The maximum number of items to keep in the pool. This defaults to 1024. This value is a recommendation, the pool may keep more objects than this.
member.remarks: On .NET 6 and above, cancellation token sources are reusable and this pool leverages this feature. When running on older frameworks, this pool is actually a no-op, every time a source is fetched from the pool, it is always a new instance. In that case, returning an object to the pool merely disposes it.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PoolFactory.SharedStringBuilderPool
member.summary: Gets the shared pool of instances.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification
member.summary: Represents a single classification which is part of a data taxonomy.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification.None
member.summary: Gets the value to represent data with no defined classification.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification.Unknown
member.summary: Gets the value to represent data with an unknown classification.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification.TaxonomyName
member.summary: Gets the name of the taxonomy that recognizes this classification.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification.Value
member.summary: Gets the value representing the classification within the taxonomy.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification.#ctor(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the struct.
member.param: The name of the data taxonomy this classification belongs to.
The taxonomy-specific value representing the classification.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification.Equals(System.Object)
member.summary: Checks if an object is equal to this instance of .
member.param: Object to check for equality.
member.returns: if object instances are equal otherwise.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification.Equals(Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification)
member.summary: Checks if an object is equal to this instance of .
member.param: Instance of to check for equality.
member.returns: if object instances are equal otherwise.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification.GetHashCode
member.summary: Get the hash code for the current instance.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification.op_Equality(Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification,Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification)
member.summary: Check if two instances are equal.
member.param: Left argument of the comparison.
Right argument of the comparison.
member.returns: if object instances are equal, or otherwise.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification.op_Inequality(Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification,Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification)
member.summary: Check if two instances are not equal.
member.param: Left argument of the comparison.
Right argument of the comparison.
member.returns: if object instances are equal, or otherwise.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification.ToString
member.summary: Gets a string representation of this object.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationAttribute
member.summary: Base attribute for data classification.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationAttribute.Notes
member.summary: Gets or sets the notes.
member.remarks: Optional free-form text to provide context during a privacy audit.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationAttribute.Classification
member.summary: Gets the classification represented by this attribute.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationAttribute.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The data classification to apply.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationSet
member.summary: Represents a set of data classifications.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationSet.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The class to include in the set.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationSet.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The classes to include in the set.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationSet.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification[])
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The classes to include in the set.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationSet.op_Implicit(Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification)~Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationSet
member.summary: Converts a single classification to a data classification set.
member.param: The classification to include in the set.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationSet.FromDataClassification(Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification)
member.summary: Converts a single classification to a data classification set.
member.param: The classification to include in the set.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationSet.Union(Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationSet)
member.summary: Creates a new data classification set that combines the current classifications with another set.
member.param: The other set.
member.remarks: This method doesn't modify the two input sets, it creates a new set.
member.returns: The resulting set which combines the current instance's classifications and the other ones.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationSet.GetHashCode
member.summary: Gets a hash code for the current object instance.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationSet.Equals(System.Object)
member.summary: Compares an object with the current instance to see if they contain the same classifications.
member.param: The other data classification to compare to.
member.returns: if the two sets contain the same classifications.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationSet.Equals(Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationSet)
member.summary: Compares an object with the current instance to see if they contain the same classifications.
member.param: The other data classification to compare to.
member.returns: if the two sets contain the same classifications.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationSet.ToString
member.summary: Returns a string representation of this object.
member.returns: The string representation of this object.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationSet.ComputeHashCode
member.summary: Computes the hash code for the current object instance.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationTypeConverter
member.summary: Provides a way to convert a to and from a string.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationTypeConverter.TryParse(System.String,System.String@,System.String@)
member.summary: Attempts to parse a string in the format "TaxonomyName:Value".
member.param: The input string to parse.
When this method returns, contains the parsed taxonomy name if the parsing succeeded, or an empty string if it failed.
When this method returns, contains the parsed taxonomy value if the parsing succeeded, or the original input string if it failed.
member.returns: if the string was successfully parsed; otherwise, .

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.NoDataClassificationAttribute
member.summary: Indicates data which is specifically not classified.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.NoDataClassificationAttribute.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.UnknownDataClassificationAttribute
member.summary: Indicates data whose classification is unknown.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.UnknownDataClassificationAttribute.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.IRedactionBuilder
member.summary: Adds redactors to the application.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.IRedactionBuilder.Services
member.summary: Gets the service collection into which the redactor instances are registered.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.IRedactionBuilder.SetRedactor``1(Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationSet[])
member.summary: Sets the redactor to use for a set of data classifications.
member.typeparam: Redactor type.
member.param: The data classifications for which the redactor type should be used.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.IRedactionBuilder.SetFallbackRedactor``1
member.summary: Sets the redactor to use when processing classified data for which no specific redactor has been registered.
member.typeparam: Redactor type.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.IRedactorProvider
member.summary: Provides redactors for different data classifications.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.IRedactorProvider.GetRedactor(Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationSet)
member.summary: Gets the redactor configured to handle the specified data class.
member.param: Data classifications of the data to redact.
member.returns: A redactor suitable to redact data of the given class.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.NullRedactor
member.summary: Redactor that does nothing to its input and returns it as-is.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.NullRedactor.Instance
member.summary: Gets the singleton instance of this class.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.NullRedactorProvider
member.summary: A provider that only returns the redactor used for situations that don't require redaction.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.NullRedactorProvider.Instance
member.summary: Gets the singleton instance of this class.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.Redactor
member.summary: Enables the redaction of potentially sensitive data.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.Redactor.Redact(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char})
member.summary: Redacts potentially sensitive data.
member.param: Value to redact.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.Redactor.Redact(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char},System.Span{System.Char})
member.summary: Redacts potentially sensitive data.
member.param: Value to redact.
Buffer to store redacted value.
member.returns: Number of characters produced when redacting the given source input.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.Redactor.Redact(System.String,System.Span{System.Char})
member.summary: Redacts potentially sensitive data.
member.param: Value to redact.
Buffer to redact into.
member.remarks: Returns 0 when is .
member.returns: Number of characters written to the buffer.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.Redactor.Redact(System.String)
member.summary: Redacts potentially sensitive data.
member.param: Value to redact.
member.remarks: Returns an empty string when is .

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.Redactor.Redact``1(``0,System.String,System.IFormatProvider)
member.summary: Redacts potentially sensitive data.
member.typeparam: Type of value to redact.
member.param: Value to redact.
The optional format that selects the specific formatting operation performed. Refer to the documentation of the type being formatted to understand the values you can supply here.
Format provider used to produce a string representing the value.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.Redactor.Redact``1(``0,System.Span{System.Char},System.String,System.IFormatProvider)
member.summary: Redacts potentially sensitive data.
member.typeparam: Type of value to redact.
member.param: Value to redact.
Buffer to redact into.
The optional format string that selects the specific formatting operation performed. Refer to the documentation of the type being formatted to understand the values you can supply here.
Format provider used to produce a string representing the value.
member.returns: Number of characters written to the buffer.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.Redactor.TryRedact``1(``0,System.Span{System.Char},System.Int32@,System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char},System.IFormatProvider)
member.summary: Tries to redact potentially sensitive data.
member.typeparam: The type of value to redact.
member.param: The value to redact.
The buffer to redact into.
When this method returns, contains the number of redacted characters that were written to the destination buffer.
The format string that selects the specific formatting operation performed. Refer to the documentation of the type being formatted to understand the values you can supply here.
The format provider used to produce a string representing the value.
member.returns: if the destination buffer was large enough, otherwise .

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.Redactor.GetRedactedLength(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char})
member.summary: Gets the number of characters produced by redacting the input.
member.param: Value to be redacted.
member.returns: The number of characters produced by redacting the input.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.Redactor.GetRedactedLength(System.String)
member.summary: Gets the number of characters produced by redacting the input.
member.param: Value to be redacted.
